title: Ordered-CTR CatBoost binary expert for high-cardinality race context
summary: The current best pipelines mostly reduce `Driver`/`Race`/interaction categories to frequency counts.
rationale: The current best pipelines mostly reduce `Driver`/`Race`/interaction categories to frequency counts. CatBoost’s ordered categorical statistics are designed to use target-aware category combinations without the usual leakage from naive target encoding, which is especially relevant here because `Driver`, `Race`, `Year_Race`, and compound/stint interactions are high-cardinality and strategy-specific.
implementation_hint: Add a separate CatBoostClassifier expert on the same numeric features plus raw categorical columns (`Driver`, `Race`, `Compound`, `Year_Race`, `Driver_Race`, `Race_Compound`, `Compound_Stint`). Keep race-blocked folds, let CatBoost handle category combinations directly, and blend its OOF probabilities with the existing GBDT baseline rather than replacing the baseline first.
expected_effect: Better use of sparse strategic identities and cross-feature interactions, especially for driver-track-specific stop tendencies that frequency encoding cannot express.
risk: If folds are not strictly race-blocked and fold-local, ordered CTR benefits can turn into hidden leakage; GPU/CPU parameter differences can also change behavior materially.
